title: ottelia lisowskii
intro: Ottelia lisowskii, also known as the water wisteria or water rot, is a semi-aquatic aquatic plant native to Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. Ottelia lisowskii is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing swelling and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Ottelia lisowskii can be made into teas or infusions, and the plant's roots can be used to create decoctions or infusions for medicinal purposes.
